extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and drops steeply to below 0.2 within the first 20% of the time.  
R2: Reaches a clear minimum valley around 0.1–0.15 roughly between 20% and 40% of the time.  
R3: Then rises to a mid–high plateau around 0.4–0.55 between about 40% and 70% of the time.  
R4: In the last 30% of the time, either stays around that mid level or rises slightly, but never clearly above ~0.6.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.7) and drops steeply, but only to about 0.25–0.35 within the first 15–20% of the time.  
R2: Continues a more gradual decline to a minimum of about 0.1–0.2 reached later than class 0, roughly between 25% and 45% of the time.  
R3: Then rises strongly to a mid–high level 0.45–0.6, reached a bit earlier than in class 0 (around 40–55% of the time).  
R4: After this rise, remains on a relatively flat mid–high plateau (0.4–0.6) with only small oscillations for the rest of the time.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 0–15), the series stays near its minimum for a relatively long, flat “valley” (roughly time 15–35) before it starts rising again.  
R2: From that valley to about the middle of the series (roughly time 35–65), the values rise to a moderate level (around 0.45–0.55) and then fluctuate gently without a strong upward or downward trend.  
R3: In the last third of the series (roughly time 65–100), values remain in the mid-range or gently rise, without a clear, smooth upward ramp from the minimum to the end.  
R4: Overall shape: “fast drop → long low plateau → rise to mid-level and then roughly horizontal / mildly varying segment.”
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around time 0–15), the series does not stay flat for long near its minimum; instead, it soon begins a smooth, sustained rise.  
R2: From near the minimum (roughly time 20–35) up to about the middle of the series (roughly time 35–60), the series shows a clear upward ramp from low values (≈0.1–0.2) to mid/high values (≈0.45–0.55) without an extended low plateau.  
R3: After this ramp (roughly time 60–100), the series stays at a mid/high level with small fluctuations, without a second, late rising phase.  
R4: Overall shape: “fast drop → brief low region → long, smooth upward ramp → mid/high plateau.”

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around index 5–15), the series remains near its minimum for a relatively long flat “valley” (roughly indices 20–40).
R2: The main upward recovery from this valley begins later (after about index 35) rather than immediately.
R3: In the second half of the series (indices 50–100), the values are generally rising or high, with no extended low plateau.
R4: The average value over the last third of the series is clearly higher than the average over the middle third.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series does not stay low for long; it begins its main upward recovery earlier (around indices 25–35).
R2: The low “valley” segment is shorter and shifted earlier compared with Class 0, with the minimum generally occurring before index 30.
R3: The recovery reaches a high plateau in the middle of the series (around indices 40–70), after which the series tends to stabilize or gently decline.
R4: The average value over the middle third of the series is similar to or higher than the average over the last third.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial sharp drop (around index 5–15), the series stays near its minimum and forms a relatively flat low plateau (indices ~15–30) instead of immediately rising.
R2: The main rise from this low is gradual and extended over many points (indices ~30–60), not a sudden spike.
R3: After this rise, the series either stays roughly flat or shows only mild undulations, without a pronounced secondary peak.
R4: There is no clear, tall bump in the middle of the series; mid‑segment values remain moderate compared to the initial peak.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial sharp drop, the series continues downward into a clear, well‑defined trough (indices ~15–30) and then starts to rise sharply.
R2: From this trough, there is a relatively steep and distinct upward movement forming a main bump/peak in the middle of the series (indices ~30–60).
R3: This middle bump is followed by a noticeable decline, so values after the peak are lower than the bump but higher than the earlier trough.
R4: Overall, the class‑1 shape is “valley then single main hill in the middle,” with that hill clearly standing out from earlier and later values.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Start with a high value (around 0.6–0.7) and drop steeply to a low value (around 0.1) within the first ~20 time steps.  
R2: After this drop, remain at a low plateau (roughly 0.1–0.2) until about time step 30–35.  
R3: From ~30–35 to ~50–60, rise to a mid-range level (around 0.4–0.5), forming a broad hump rather than a sharp peak.  
R4: In the last third of the series, stay roughly in the mid-range (0.35–0.55) with only mild fluctuations, without a second pronounced dip.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Start high and drop sharply, then briefly rebound to a mid value (around 0.35–0.45), followed by another clear decline, so the early segment shows a “high–mid–low” pattern instead of a single monotone drop.  
R2: Reach a clear minimum (around 0.1) later than class 0, typically around time step 25–30 rather than before 20.  
R3: From this later minimum, rise more steeply to a mid–high level (around 0.45–0.55) between about time steps 35–50.  
R4: In the last third of the series, stay roughly at this mid–high level (around 0.4–0.55) with small fluctuations, without the early long low plateau characteristic of class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial sharp drop from a high value (~0.7–0.8), the series reaches its minimum (around 0.1–0.15) relatively early, roughly between indices 20–40.  
R2: From this early minimum, the series rises and forms a broad hump peaking around 0.45–0.55 approximately between indices 35–60.  
R3: After this hump, the series does not fall back to very low values; it stays mostly above ~0.35 for the rest of the series.  
R4: In the last quarter of the series (indices ~70–100), the values are generally higher than in the early minimum, typically in the range 0.4–0.6 with a slight upward or flat trend.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an initial sharp drop from a high value (~0.7–0.9), the series continues downward and reaches a deeper minimum (around 0.05–0.12) later than class 0, roughly between indices 30–50.  
R2: From this later and deeper minimum, the series rises steeply to a peak around 0.45–0.55 occurring between indices 45–70.  
R3: After this peak, the series flattens or gently declines, staying mostly around 0.35–0.5 without a strong upward trend toward the very end.  
R4: Compared to class 0, the late part of the series (indices ~70–100) remains relatively flat or slightly decreasing and does not show a clear final increase from ~0.4 toward ~0.6.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After the initial peak and steep drop (around index 0–15), the series forms a broad “valley” whose minimum is very low (roughly near 0.1).  
R2: From this valley minimum (around index 25–35), the series rises steadily over a long segment.  
R3: In the last third of the series, the values stay at a medium-to-high level (around 0.4–0.55) and often end slightly higher than the valley’s mid-level.  
R4: There is no strong, isolated secondary peak in the middle; the mid-section is relatively smooth and plateau-like rather than forming a sharp hump.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After the initial peak and steep drop (around index 0–15), the series also forms a valley, but its minimum is slightly higher on average than in Class 0 or the descent into it is less smooth (more jagged).  
R2: From this valley minimum, the series rises quickly into a pronounced “middle hump” (around index 35–55) that clearly stands out relative to both the earlier valley and the later segment.  
R3: The last third of the series (after the middle hump) remains at a moderate level, typically not rising above the middle hump and often gently declining or flattening.  
R4: Overall, the most prominent sustained high region is in the middle of the series rather than near the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.8) and drops steeply to below 0.2 within the first ~20 time steps.  
R2: Reaches its minimum (≈0.1) early (before ~35) and then rises to a mid‑segment plateau around 0.4–0.5.  
R3: In the middle third (roughly time 30–70) stays mostly between 0.35 and 0.55 with only moderate fluctuations.  
R4: In the final third shows a renewed upward trend, ending higher than the early minimum (typically above 0.4).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.9) and drops steeply, but after this drop the values remain clearly higher than the deepest minima of class 0 (generally above ≈0.15).  
R2: Has an early local minimum and then a clear mid‑segment rise forming a pronounced hump/plateau roughly between 0.4 and 0.6.  
R3: After this mid‑segment peak, remains relatively flat or gently decreasing without a strong second rise near the end.  
R4: Ends at a level similar to or slightly below the mid‑segment plateau (around 0.35–0.5), without a strong late upswing.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: After an initial sharp drop, the series stays near its minimum (low, almost flat region) for a relatively long middle segment.
R2: From this minimum, the series shows a gradual, roughly monotonic increase toward the end.
R3: In the last quarter of the series, values are clearly higher than around the minimum but do not exceed the very early peak.
R4: The overall shape is “U-like”: early high peak → long low plateau/valley → late rise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: After an initial sharp drop, the series reaches its minimum quickly and does not remain in a long flat low plateau.
R2: From this early minimum, the series rises more steeply and earlier in time than in Class 0.
R3: The middle portion of the series (roughly central third) is dominated by a single broad hump at medium-to-high values, not a long valley.
R4: The overall shape is more “V then hump”: early peak → quick dip → early recovery to a sustained mid-level plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.8) and drops steeply to a clear minimum below 0.2 within the first 30% of the series.  
R2: After this minimum, rises to a broad mid-level plateau around 0.4–0.55 by the middle of the series.  
R3: In the last 30% of the series, either stays roughly flat or increases further, ending at or above 0.4.  
R4: Has only one pronounced early valley; afterwards no deep dips below 0.2.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Starts high (around 0.6–0.8) but, after the initial peak, declines less smoothly, with an early secondary bump or shoulder before reaching its minimum.  
R2: The minimum still occurs within the first 30–35% of the series and drops below 0.2.  
R3: After the minimum, rises to a mid-level plateau around 0.4–0.55 but then shows a slight downward or flat trend toward the end, finishing near 0.35–0.45 without a strong late increase.  
R4: Compared with Class 0, shows more local fluctuations (small ups and downs) in the middle and late parts of the series rather than a smoother single rise.
